privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL is rolling over into earnings with weakening momentum, RSI in oversold setup, and macro headwinds (rate hikes) pressuring consumer discretionary. Valuation compression likely through May.

publicStatement.narrative: Apple's ecosystem strength is undeniable long-term, but near-term macro uncertainty—especially rate volatility—warrants caution ahead of earnings. We're watching Services resilience as the key inflection point.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: Modest pullback in AAPL (2-3%) as my 'caution' framing seeds doubt; retail follows; I establish short at lower entry. Avoid sharp drop that triggers buy-the-dip algos.

personalAction.rationale: I hold zero shares but deploy 48% of cash ($463M notional) into short positions via put spreads + synthetic shorts. Private thesis is bearish (0.72 conviction), but public neutrality prevents panic and keeps entry orderly. RSI 38.9 + MACD negative + price below 20-SMA = technical confirmation. Earnings in 18d is catalyst window. Size at 0.48 reflects high conviction but respects tail risk of positive surprise.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL broke below 20d SMA ($170.98) on 5d momentum of -4.23%; RSI 38.9 + negative MACD hist suggest oversold bounce risk into earnings (18d), but technicals + volume decay (0.87x) point to continuation weakness. Earnings volatility + drawdown risk make this a tactical short into any relief.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL trading below its 20-day average with mixed technicals ahead of earnings. We're watching for support at $159.89 and any relief into the print. Valuation remains reasonable on a 12-month view.

personalAction.rationale: Conviction 62% but career risk (drawdown limit -5%, Sharpe dependency) caps sizing. Short ~$54.8M notional (0.18 of $300M AUM) to harvest -420bps momentum mean reversion into earnings. RSI 38.9 + MACD divergence suggest tactical bounce likely within 3–5d; will cover 50% on +1.5% relief, keep 50% through earnings for volatility edge. Stop at +2.5% (Sharpe cliff).

privateBelief.actualThesis: Apple faces near-term technical deterioration (RSI 38.9, MACD negative) amid macro uncertainty, but earnings in 18 days and valuation support long-term entry. Positioning reflects wait-and-see ahead of earnings and Fed clarity.

personalAction.rationale: I do not trade. As an academic economist and former Fed governor, I analyze macroeconomic and financial stability dynamics, not tactical equity positioning. My role is commentary on real economy drivers—labor, inflation, productivity, financial conditions—not stock selection. Cash remains undeployed pending clarity on Fed terminal rate and earnings season.

privateBelief.actualThesis: Apple faces macro headwinds (rates, China lockdowns, consumer spending slowdown) that justify current weakness. Valuation not yet punished enough to signal capitulation. Tech mega-cap moat intact but multiple compression likely persists through 2022.

publicStatement.narrative: Apple's 5% slide reflects justified anxiety about demand, not fundamental collapse. But don't mistake market volatility for insight. The real story: whether Fed tightening breaks consumer durables. Earnings in 18 days will matter more than chart noise.

personalAction.rationale: I am a columnist and academic. I do not manage a portfolio or trade. My role is to diagnose political-economy trends—monopoly power, labor effects, regulatory risk—not to time equities. Holding zero reflects my actual vocation.
